channel.title: kubernetes on Aiden's Laboratory
channel.description: Recent content in kubernetes on Aiden's Laboratory
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2020-2021, Jongseob Jeon; all rights reserved.

item.title: \[CKA\] 06. Security
item.description:
Kubernetes Security Primitives

Secrure Hosts

  • Password based authentication disabled
  • SSH Key based authentication

Secure kubernetes

  • kube-apiserver
    • Who can access?
    • What can they do?

Authentication

Who can access?

  • Files - Username and Passwords
  • Fiels - Username and Tokens
  • Certificates
  • External Authenication providers - LDAP
  • Service Accounts

Authorization

What can they do?

  • RBAC Authorization
  • ABAC Authorization
  • Node Authorization
  • Webhook Mode

TLS Certificates

Network Policies

Authentication

Users

  • Admins
  • Developers
  • Application End Users
  • Bots

Accounts

  • User
    • Admins
    • Developers
  • Servcie Accounts
    • Bots
    • not supported officialy in k8s

Auth Mechanisms

  • kube-apiserver
    • Static Password File
    • Static Token File
    • Certificates
    • Identity Service

Basic - file

  • user-details.csv 
    1password123,user1,u0001
2password123,user2,u0002
  • kube-apiserver.service 
    1ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/kube-apiserver \\\\
2 --basic-auth-file=user-details.csvc \\\\
3 ...
  • kubeadm 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 creationTimestamp: null
 5 name: kube-apiserver
 6 namespace: kube-system
 7spec:
 8 containers:
 9 - command:
10 - kube-apiserver
11 - --authorization-mode=Node,RBAC
12 <content-hidden>
13 - --basic-auth-file=/tmp/users/user-details.csv
  • pod 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: kube-apiserver
 5 namespace: kube-system
 6spec:
 7 containers:
 8 - command:
 9 - kube-apiserver
10 <content-hidden>
11 image: k8s.gcr.io/kube-apiserver-amd64:v1.11.3
12 name: kube-apiserver
13 volumeMounts:
14 - mountPath: /tmp/users
15 name: usr-details
16 readOnly: true
17 volumes:
18 - hostPath:
19 path: /tmp/users
20 type: DirectoryOrCreate
21 name: usr-details
  • Authenticate User
    • curl -v -k <https://master-node-ip:6443/api/v1/pods> -u "user1:password123"

Basic - token

  • user-token-details.csv 
    1asdfbasdhjasdhjkfhjk12312,user1,u0001
21234g23rgy8werfg89asdfasd,user2,u0002
  • kube-apiserver.service 
    1ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/kube-apiserver \\\\
2 --token-auth-file=user-details.csvc \\\\
3 ...
  • kubeadm
    • kubectl edit
  • Authenticate User
    • curl -v -k <https://master-node-ip:6443/api/v1/pods> --header "Authorization: Bearer asdfbasdhjasdhjkfhjk12312

TLS

  • master node
  • worker nodes

Server Certificates for Servers

Kube-Api server

  • exposes a service that other components as well as external users use to manage the k8s cluster
  • server
    • certificates to secure all communications with its clients
    • apiserver.crt
    • apiserver.key

ETCD Server

  • etcdserver.crt
  • etcdserver.key

KUBELET Server

  • kubelet.crt
  • kubelet.key

Client Certificates for Clients

Admin

  • admin.crt
  • admin.key

Kube-Scheduler

  • scheduler.crt
  • scheduler.key

Kube-Controller-Manager

  • controll-manager.crt
  • controll-manager.key

Kube-Proxy

  • kube-proxy.crt
  • kube-proxy.key

Certification Creation

OpenSSL to Create Client Certification

  • Certificate Authority(CA)
    • Generate Keys (ca.key)
      • openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048
    • Certificate Signing Requests (ca.csr)
      • openssl req -new -key ca.key -subj "/CN=KUBERNETES-CA" -out ca.csr
    • Sign Certificates (ca.crt)
      • openssl x509 -req -in ca.csr -signkey ca.key -out ca.crt
  • Admin User
    • Generate Keys (admin.key)
      • openssl genrsa -out admin.key 2048
    • Certificate Signing Requests (admin.csr)
      • openssl req -new -key admin.key -subj "/CN=kube-admin/O=system:masters" -out admin.csr
      • "/CN=kube-admin/O=system:masters" ← admin user
    • Sign Certificates (admin.crt)
      • openssl x509 -req -in admin.csr -signkey ca.key -out admin.crt
  • Kube Scheduler
  • Kube Controller Manager
  • kube Proxy

How to use

  • curl <https://kube-apisever:6443/api/v1/pods> --key admin.key --cert admin.crt --cacert ca.crt
  • definition 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2clusters:
 3- cluster:
 4 certificate-authority: ca.crt
 5 server: <https://kube-apiserver:6443>
 6 name: kubernetes
 7kind: Config
 8users:
 9- name: kubernetes-admin
10 user:
11 client-certificate: admin.crt
12 clinet-key: admin.key

OpenSSL to Create Server Certification

ETCD Servers

1- etcd
2 --key-file=
3 --cert-file=
4 --peer-cert-file=
5 --peer-client-cert-auth=
6 --peer-key-file=
7 --peer-trusted-ca-cile
8 --trusted-ca-file=

Kube-Api Server

  • Generate Keys (apiserver.key)
    • openssl genrsa -out apiserver.key 2048
  • Certificate Signing Requests (apiserver.csr)
    • openssl.cnf 
       1[req]
 2req_extensions = v3_req
 3[ v3_req ]
 4basicConstraints = CA:FALSE
 5keyUsage = nonRpudiation,
 6subjectAltName = @alt_names
 7[alt_names]
 8DNS.1 = kubernetes
 9DNS.2 = kubernetes.default
10DNS.3 = kubernetes.default.svc
11DNS.4 = kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local
12IP.1 = 10.96.0.1
13IP.2 = 172.17.0.87
    • openssl req -new -key apiserver.key -subj "/CN=kube-apiserver" -out apiserver.csr -config openssl.cnf
  • Sign Certificates (apiserver.crt)
    • openssl x509 -req -in apiserver.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -out apiserver.crt

Kubelet Server

  • server cert
    • definition 
       1apiVersion: kubelet.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
 2kind: KubeletConfiguration
 3authentication:
 4 x509:
 5 clientCAFile: "/var/lib/kubernetes/ca.pem"
 6authorization:
 7 mode: Webhook
 8clusterDomain: "cluster.local"
 9clusterDNS:
10 - "10.32.0.10"
11podCIDR: "$(POD_CIDR)"
12resolveConf: "/run/systemd/resolve/resolv.conf"
13reuntimeRequrestTimeout: "15m"
14tlsCertFile: "/var/lib/kubelet/kubelet-node01.crt"
15tlsPrivateKeyFile: "/var/lib/kubelet/kubelet-node01.key"
  • client cert

View Certificate Details

  • The Hard Way
    • cat /etc/systemd/system/kube-apiserver.service
  • kubeadm
    • cat /etc/kubernetes/manifest/kube-apiserver.yaml

Inspect Service Logs

  • journalctl -u etcd.service

View Logs

  • kubectl logs etcd-master

Certificates API

Certificate Signing Request

Order

  1. Create CertificateSigningReqeust Object
  2. Review Requests
  3. Approve Requests
  4. Share Certs to Users

Request

  • openssl genrsa -out jan.key 2048
  • openssl req -new -key jane.key -subj "/CN=jane" -out jan.csr
  • send to the administrator
  • administrator takes the key and creates a CertifacteSigningRequest obejct 
     1apiVersion: certificates.k8s.io/v1beta1
 2kind: CeritifcateSigningRequest
 3metadata:
 4 name: jane
 5spec:
 6 groups:
 7 - system:authenticated
 8 usage:
 9 - digital signature
10 - key encipherment
11 - server auth
12 request:
13 <cat jane.csr | base64>
    • cat akshay.csr | base64 | tr -d "\\n"

Approve

  • kubectl get csr 
    1> k get csr
2NAME AGE SIGNERNAME REQUESTOR CONDITION
3akshay 10s kubernetes.io/kube-apiserver-client kubernetes-admin Pending
  • kubectl certificate approve jane

상세 요청

  • kubectl get csr jane -0 yaml

Deny

  • kubectl certificate deny agent-smith

Delete

  • kubectl delete csr agent-smith

Contoller Manager

  • CSR-APPROVING
  • CSR-SIGNING
  • cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-controller-manager.yaml

Kubeconfig

Using kubectl with crt

1kubectl get pods
2 --server my-kube-playground:6443
3 --client-key admin.key
4 --client-certificated admin.crt
5 --certificate-authority ca.crt
  • this is tedious task
    • → move it to config file

Using kubectl with config file

  • config 
    1 --server my-kube-playground:6443
2 --client-key admin.key
3 --client-certificated admin.crt
4 --certificate-authority ca.crt
  • kubectl
    • kubectl get pods --kubeconfig config
    • default config file path
      • .kube/config

KubeConfig File

  • clusters
    • development
    • production
    • google
    • --server my-kube-playground:6443
  • contexts
    • match cluster and users
      • admin@production
      • dev@google
      • mykubeadmin@mykubeplayground
  • users
    • admin
    • dev user
    • prod user
    1 --client-key admin.key
2 --client-certificated admin.crt
3 --certificate-authority ca.crt
  • defintion 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Config
 3current-context: dev-user@google
 4clusters:
 5- name: my-kube-playground
 6 cluster:
 7 certificate-authority: ca.crt
 8 server: my-kube-playground:6443
 9- name: ...
10contexts:
11- name: my-kube-admin@my-kube-playground
12 context:
13 clusters: my-kube-playground
14 user: my-kube-admin
15- name: ...
16users:
17- name: my-kube-admin
18 user:
19 client-certificate: admin.crt
20 client-key: admin.key
21- name: ...
    • current-context
      • default context
    • certificate-authority
      • instead file path using data directly
      • certificate-authority-data
        • with base64 encoding

Command Line Tool

  • view
    • kubectl config view
    • kubectl config view --kubeconfig=<config-file-name>
  • update
    • kubectl config use-context prod-user@production
  • helo
    • kubectl config -h

API Groups

Example

  • curl <https://kube-master:6443/version>
  • curl <https://kube-master:6443/api/v1/pods>

Types

  • /metrics
  • /healthz
  • /version
  • /api
  • /apis
  • /logs

API, APIS

  • api
    • core group
  • apis
    • named group
  • CLT
    • curl <http://localhost:6443> -k
    • curl <http://localhost:6443> -k | grep "name"
    • need certificates file
  • kubectl proxy
    • to see proxy
    • kube proxy ≠ kubectl proxy
    • kube proxy
      • enable connectivity betewwnd pods and services across different nodes in the cluster.
    • kubectl proxy
      • HTTP proxy service created by kubectl utility to access the kube-api sever

Authorization

Authorization Mechanisms

Types

  • Node
  • ABAC
  • RBAC
  • Webhook

Node

  • Node Authorizer

ABAC

User Attribute Based Authorization

  • dev-user
    • → Can view PODs
    • → Can create PODs
    • → Can delete PODs
    1{"kind": "Policy", "spec": {"user": "dev-user", "namespace": "*", "resource": "pods", "apiGroup": "*"}}

RBAC

Rule Based Authorizaion

  • Developer
    • → Can view PODs
    • → Can create PODs
    • → Can delete PODs
  • definition 
    1apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
2kind: Role
3metadata:
4 name: developer
5rules:
6- apiGroups: [""]
7 resources: ["pods"]
8 verbs: ["list", "get", "create", "update", "delete"]
9 resourceNames: ["blue", "orange"]
    • rules have 3 sections
      • apiGroups
      • resources
      • verbs
  • link users to rule 
     1apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
 2kind: RoleBinding
 3metadata:
 4 name: devuser-developer-binding
 5subjects:
 6- kind: User
 7 name: dev-user
 8 apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
 9roleRef:
10 kind: Role
11 name: developer
12 apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  • view
    • kubectl get roles
    • kubectl get rolebindings
  • Check Access
    • kubectl auth can-i create deployments
    • kubectl auth can-i delete nodes
    • kubectl auth can-i create deployments --as dev-user

Webhook

  • outsource all the mechanism

AlwaysAllow

  • always allow

AlwaysDeny

  • always deny

Setting

1ExecStart = ...\\
2 --authorization-mode=AlwaysAllow
  • default : AlwaysAllow
  • multiple mode: 
    1Node,RBAC,Webhook
    • if one's requiest is denied, it pass to next chain
    • if approved no more checks.

Inspect

  • 1kubectl describe pod kube-apisever-controlplane -n kube-system
    • --authorizastion mode

Cluster Roles

Types

  • namespace ← role
    • pods
    • replicasets
    • ...
    • kubectl api-resources --namespaced=true
  • cluster scoped
    • nodes
    • pv
    • ...
    • kubectl api-resources --namespaced=false

Clusterroles

  • Cluster admin
    • Can view Nodes
    • can create Nodes
    • Can delete Nodes
  • definition 
    1apiVersion: rbac.authorizastion.k8s.io/v1
2kind: ClusterRole
3metadata:
4 name: cluster-administrator
5rules:
6- apiGroups: [""]
7 resources: ["nodes"]
8 verbs: ["list", "get", "create", "delete"]

Clusterrolebining

  • definition 
     1apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
 2kind: ClusterRoleBinding
 3metadata:
 4 name: cluster-admin-role-binding
 5subjects:
 6- kind: User
 7 name: cluster-admin
 8 apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
 9roleRef:
10 kind: ClusterRole
11 name: cluster-administrator
12 apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

Image Security

Private Repository

Login to private repository

  • docker login private-registry.io
  • docker run private-registry.io/apss/internal-app

Create secret

1kubectl create secrete docker-registry regcred \\
2 --docker-server=private-registry.io \\
3 --docker-username=registry-user \\
4 --docker-password=registry-password \\
5 --docker-email=registry-user@org.com

Create pod

  • definition 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Poid
 3metadata:
 4 name: nginx-pod
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: nginx
 8 image: private-registry.op/apps/internal-app
 9 imagePullSecrets:
10 - name: regcred

Security Context

  • definition for pod 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: web-pod
 5spec:
 6 securityContext:
 7 runAsUser: 1000
 8 containers:
 9 - name: ubuntu
10 image: ubuntu
11 command: ["sleep", "3600"]
  • definition for container 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: web-pod
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: ubuntu
 8 image: ubuntu
 9 command: ["sleep", "3600"]
10 securityContext:
11 runAsUser: 1000
12 capabilities:
13 add: ["AMC_ADMIN"]
    • capabilities
      • only supported at the container level

Network Policy

Ingress & Egress

  • ingress
    • for a web serever the incoming traffic from the users is an ingress
  • egress
    • outgoing request to the app server
    • allow external calls

traffic

Network Security

  • Network Policy
    • eg) Allow Ingress Raffic From API pod on Port 3306

Selectors

  • definition 
     1apiVersions: networking.k8s.io/v1
 2kind: NetworkPolicy
 3metadata:
 4 name: db-policy
 5spec:
 6 podSelector:
 7 mathLabels:
 8 role: db
 9 policyTpes:
10 - Ingress
11 ingress:
12 - from:
13 - podSelector:
14 matchLabels:
15 name: api-pod
16 ports:
17 - protocol: TCP
18 port: 3306

Network Policies

  • network policy to pod to protect
    • defintion 
      1apiVersions: networking.k8s.io/v1
2kind: NetworkPolicy
3metadata:
4 name: db-policy
5spec:
6 podSelector:
7 mathLabels:
8 role: db
  • what type of policies?
    • ingress or egress or both ?
    • allow incoming request → ingress
    • if incoming request is allowed, response is allowed automatically
    • definition 
      1 policyTpes:
2 - Ingress
  • define specific rules 
    1 ingress:
2 - from:
3 - podSelector:
4 matchLabels:
5 name: api-pod
6 ports:
7 - protocol: TCP
8 port: 3306
  • allow only in prod namespace
    • definition (and) 
       1 ingress:
 2 - from:
 3 - podSelector:
 4 matchLabels:
 5 name: api-pod
 6 namespaceSelector:
 7 matchLabels:
 8 name: prod
 9 ports:
10 - protocol: TCP
11 port: 3306
      • if only namespaceSelector is defined
        • allow or request in same namespace
    • definition (or) 
       1 ingress:
 2 - from:
 3 - podSelector:
 4 matchLabels:
 5 name: api-pod
 6 - namespaceSelector:
 7 matchLabels:
 8 name: prod
 9 ports:
10 - protocol: TCP
11 port: 3306
      • use array
  • to backup in server 
    1 - ipBlock:
2 - cidr: 192.168.5.10/32
  • to push to bakcup server 
     1apiVersions: networking.k8s.io/v1
 2kind: NetworkPolicy
 3metadata:
 4 name: db-policy
 5spec:
 6 podSelector:
 7 mathLabels:
 8 role: db
 9 policyTpes:
10 - Ingress
11 - Egress
12 ingress:
13 - from:
14 - podSelector:
15 matchLabels:
16 name: api-pod
17 ports:
18 - protocol: TCP
19 port: 3306
20 egress:
21 - to:
22 - ipBlock:
23 cidr: 192.168.5.10/32
24 ports:
25 - protocol: TCP
26 port: 80

item.title: \[CKA\] 07. Storage
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

Docker Storage

File system

Layered architecture

  • build
    • application 1
    • application 2
  • run
  • copy-on-write

Volumes

Volume mounting

  • create and run
    • create
      • docker volume create data_volume
    • run
      • docker run -v data_volume:/var/lib/mysql mysql
  • create by run
    • docker run -v data_volume2:/var/lib/mysql mysql
    • automatically create data_volume

Bind mounting

  • docker run -v /data/mysql:/var/lib/mysql mysql

Preferred

  • old
    • -v
  • new
    • --mount
    • docker run --mount type=bind,source=/data/mysql,target=/var/lib/mysql mysql

Storage drivers

  • AUFS
  • ZFS
  • BTFS
  • Device Mapper
  • Overlay
  • Overlay2

Volume Drivers

  • Local
  • Azure File Storage
  • Convoy
  • ...

Volumes

  • docker
  • k8s

Volumes & Mounts

  • definition

     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadat:
 4 name: random-number-generator
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - image: alpine
 8 name: alpine
 9 command: ["bin/sh", "-c"]
10 args: ["shuf -i 0-100 -n 1 >> /opt/number.out;"]
11 volumeMounts:
12 - mountPath: /opt
13 name: data-volume
14 volumes:
15 - name: data-volume
16 hostPath:
17 path: /data
18 type: Directory
    • hostPath
      • → single node is possible
      • → not recommended in multi node
    • AWS 
      1 volumes:
2 - name: data-volume
3 awsElastricBlockStore:
4 volumeID: <volume-id>
5 fsType: ext4

Persistent Volume

생성

  • definition 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: PersistentVolume
 3metadata:
 4 name: pv-vol1
 5spec:
 6 persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Retain
 7 accessModes:
 8 - ReadWriteOnce
 9 capacity:
10 storage: 1Gi
11 hostPath:
12 path: /tmp/data
    • accessModes
      • ReadOnlyMany
      • ReadWriteOnce
      • ReadWriteMany
    • persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy
      • delete
      • recycle
      • retain

상태

  • kubectl get persistentvolume
  • kubectl get pv

Persistent Volume Claims

  • Persistent Volume
    • administrator crates
  • Persistent Volume Claims
    • users create to use persistent volume

생성

  • definition 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
 3metadata:
 4 name: myclaim
 5spec:
 6 accessModes:
 7 - ReadWriteOnce
 8 resources:
 9 requests:
10 storage: 500Mi

상태

  • kubectl get persistentvolumeclaim
  • kubectl get pvc

삭제

  • kubectl delete persistentvolumne myclaim

Pod with PVC

생성

  • definition w\ hostPath 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: webapp
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: event-simulator
 8 image: kodekloud/event-simulator
 9 env:
10 - name: LOG_HANDLERS
11 value: file
12 volumeMounts:
13 - mountPath: /log
14 name: log-volume
15 volumes:
16 - name: log-volume
17 hostPath:
18 ## directory location on host
19 path: /var/log/webapp
20 ## this field is optional
21 type: Directory
  • definition w\ PVC 
     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: webapp
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: event-simulator
 8 image: kodekloud/event-simulator
 9 env:
10 - name: LOG_HANDLERS
11 value: file
12 volumeMounts:
13 - mountPath: /log
14 name: log-volume
15
16 volumes:
17 - name: log-volume
18 persistentVolumeClaim:
19 claimName: claim-log-1

Storage Class

상태

  • kubectl get storageclass
  • kubectl get sc

item.title: \[CKA\] 05. Cluster Maintenance
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

Operating System Upgrade

  • upgrading base software
  • applying security patches

Pod Eviction Timeout

  • waiting pod healthy
    • pod-eviction-timeout=5m0s

Drain

Do not use this node, and all pods are out.

  • kubectl drain node01
  • kubectl drain node01 --ignore-daemonsets 
     1root@controlplane:~# kubectl drain node01 --ignore-daemonsets
 2node/node01 cordoned
 3WARNING: ignoring DaemonSet-managed Pods: kube-system/kube-flannel-ds-x6dgs, kube-system/kube-proxy-jfmxw
 4evicting pod default/blue-746c87566d-wn2qg
 5evicting pod default/blue-746c87566d-kt266
 6evicting pod default/blue-746c87566d-9w9zq
 7pod/blue-746c87566d-kt266 evicted
 8pod/blue-746c87566d-wn2qg evicted
 9pod/blue-746c87566d-9w9zq evicted
10node/node01 evicted
  • kubectl drain node01 --ignore-daemonsets --force
    if a pod in node has no replicaset, use --force option, but a pod will be lost. 
    1root@controlplane:~# kubectl drain node01 --ignore-daemonsets --force
2node/node01 already cordoned
3WARNING: deleting Pods not managed by ReplicationController, ReplicaSet, Job, DaemonSet or StatefulSet: default/hr-app; ignoring DaemonSet-managed Pods: kube-system/kube-flannel-ds-x6dgs, kube-system/kube-proxy-jfmxw
4evicting pod default/hr-app
5pod/hr-app evicted
6node/node01 evicted

Cordon

Do not use this node, but exist pods will be running.

  • kubectl cordon node01
    • This will ensure that no new pods are scheduled on this node.
    • The existing pods will not be affected by this operation.

Uncordon

Can use this node.

  • kubectl uncordon node01

Cluster Upgrade Process

Supported versions

gcp

kubeadm

  • kubectl upgrade plan

     1root@controlplane:~## kubeadm upgrade plan
 2[upgrade/config] Making sure the configuration is correct:
 3[upgrade/config] Reading configuration from the cluster...
 4[upgrade/config] FYI: You can look at this config file with 'kubectl -n kube-system get cm kubeadm-config -oyaml'
 5[preflight] Running pre-flight checks.
 6[upgrade] Running cluster health checks
 7[upgrade] Fetching available versions to upgrade to
 8[upgrade/versions] Cluster version: v1.19.0
 9[upgrade/versions] kubeadm version: v1.19.0
10I0522 08:54:38.911002 21647 version.go:252] remote version is much newer: v1.21.1; falling back to: stable-1.19
11[upgrade/versions] Latest stable version: v1.19.11
12[upgrade/versions] Latest stable version: v1.19.11
13[upgrade/versions] Latest version in the v1.19 series: v1.19.11
14[upgrade/versions] Latest version in the v1.19 series: v1.19.11
15
16Components that must be upgraded manually after you have upgraded the control plane with 'kubeadm upgrade apply':
17COMPONENT CURRENT AVAILABLE
18kubelet 2 x v1.19.0 v1.19.11
19
20Upgrade to the latest version in the v1.19 series:
21
22COMPONENT CURRENT AVAILABLE
23kube-apiserver v1.19.0 v1.19.11
24kube-controller-manager v1.19.0 v1.19.11
25kube-scheduler v1.19.0 v1.19.11
26kube-proxy v1.19.0 v1.19.11
27CoreDNS 1.7.0 1.7.0
28etcd 3.4.9-1 3.4.9-1
29
30You can now apply the upgrade by executing the following command:
31
32 kubeadm upgrade apply v1.19.11
33
34Note: Before you can perform this upgrade, you have to update kubeadm to v1.19.11.
35
36_____________________________________________________________________
37
38The table below shows the current state of component configs as understood by this version of kubeadm.
39Configs that have a "yes" mark in the "MANUAL UPGRADE REQUIRED" column require manual config upgrade or
40resetting to kubeadm defaults before a successful upgrade can be performed. The version to manually
41upgrade to is denoted in the "PREFERRED VERSION" column.
42
43API GROUP CURRENT VERSION PREFERRED VERSION MANUAL UPGRADE REQUIRED
44kubeproxy.config.k8s.io v1alpha1 v1alpha1 no
45kubelet.config.k8s.io v1beta1 v1beta1 no
46_____________________________________________________________________

  • kubectl upgrade apply

Kubeadm

Strategy

  • strategy-1
    • all node down and up
  • strategy-2
    • upgrade node one by one
  • strategy-3
    • add new version node and remove old node
    • easy in cluster

Procedure

  • master node
    • ver 1
      • apt-get upgrade -y kuebadm=1.12.0-00
      • kubeadm upgrade apply v1.12.0
      • apt-get upgrade -y kubelet=1.12.0-00
      • systemctl restart kubelet
    • ver 2
      • apt update
      • apt install kubeadm=1.20.0-00
      • kubeadm upgrade apply v1.20.0
      • apt install kubelet=1.20.0-00
      • systemctl restart kubelet
  • worker node
    • ver 1
      • kubectl drain node01
      • apt-get upgrade -y kuebadm=1.12.0-00
      • apt-get upgrade -y kubelet=1.12.0-00
      • kubeadm upgrade node config --kubelet-version v1.12.0
      • systemctl restart kubelet
      • kubectl uncordon node01
    • ver 2
      • apt update
      • apt install kubeadm=1.20.0-00
      • kubeadm upgrade node
      • apt install kubelet=1.20.0-00
      • systemctl restart kubelet

Backup and Restore

Backup Candidates

  • Resource Configuration
  • ETCD Cluster
  • Persistent Volumes

Resource Configuration

  • kube-apiserver
    • kubectl get all -A -o yaml > all-deploy-svc.yaml
    • too many resource to do
    • → opensource like VELERO

ETCD

  • method 1

    • ExecStart= ... \\ --data-dir=/var/lib/etct

  • method 2

    • ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot save snapshot.db
    • servcie kube-apiserver stop
    • ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl snapshot --data-dir /var/lib/etcd-from-backup snapshot restore snapshot.db
    • systemctl daemon-reload
    • service etcd restart
    • service kube-apiserver start

  • backup practice

    1ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --endpoints=https://[127.0.0.1]:2379 \\
2--cacert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/ca.crt \\
3--cert=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.crt \\
4--key=/etc/kubernetes/pki/etcd/server.key \\
5snapshot save /opt/snapshot-pre-boot.db

  • restore practice

    • Restore snapshot 
      1ETCDCTL_API=3 etcdctl --data-dir /var/lib/etcd-from-backup \\
2snapshot restore /opt/snapshot-pre-boot.db
    • update the /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml 
      1volumes:
2 - hostPath:
3 path: /var/lib/etcd-from-backup
4 type: DirectoryOrCreate
5 name: etcd-data

item.title: \[CKA\] 04. Application Lifecycle Management
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

Rollout and Versioning

Rollout Command

  • kubectl rollout status deployment/myapp-deployment
  • kubectl rollout history deployment/myapp-deployment

Deployment Strategy

Recreate

  • destruction 5
  • create 5

Rolling Update

→ default strategy

  • destruction and create one by one

Revision

  • kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml
  • kubectl set image deployment/myapp-deployment nginx=nginx:1.9.1

Upgrades

  • make new replicaset when upgrade deploy

Rollback

  • kubectl rollout undo deployment/myapp-deployment
  • before rollback vs after rollback

Commands and Arguments

  • docker

    1From Ubuntu
2Entrypoint ["sleep"]
3cmd ["5"]

  • definition

     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: ubuntu-sleeper-pod
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: ubuntu-sleeper
 8 image: ubuntu-sleeper
 9 command: ["sleep2.0"]
10 args: ["10"]

Environment

  • plain Key Value

    1env:
2 - name: APP_COLOR
3 value: pink

  • configMap

    1env:
2 - name: APP_COLOR
3 valueFrom:
4 configMapKeyRef:

  • Secrets

    1env:
2 - name: APP_COLOR
3 valueFrom:
4 secretKeyRef:

ConfigMaps

  • create ConfigMap
  • Inject into pod

create

  • imperative

    1kubectl create configmap\\
2 app-config --from-literal=APP_COLOR=blue \\
3 --from-literal=APP_MODE=prod
    • kubectl create configmap app-config --from-file=<path-to-file>

  • declartive

    1apiVersion: v1
2kind: ConfigMap
3metadata:
4 name: app-config
5data:
6 APP_COLOR: blue
7 APP_MODE: prod

view

  • kubectl get configmaps
  • kubectl describe configmaps

ConfigMap in Pods

 1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: simple-webapp-color
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: simple-webapp-color
 8 image: simple-webapp-color
 9 envFrom:
10 - configMapRef:
11 name: app-config

Secrets

  • create secret
  • inject into pod

create

  • imperative

    1kubectl create secret generic\\
2 <secret-name> --from-literal=<key>=<value>
    • kubectl create secret <secret-name> --from-file=<path-to-file>

  • declartive

    1apiVersion: v1
2kind: Secret
3metadata:
4 name: app-secret
5data:
6 DB_Host: mysql
7 DB_User: root
8 DB_Passwird: paswrd
    • data → encoded format for safe
    • echo -n 'mysql' | base64

view

  • kubectl get secrets
  • kubectl describe secrets

decode

  • echo -n 'abalksdfas=' | base54 --decode

Secrets in Pods

  • defintion

     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: simple-webapp-color
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: simple-webapp-color
 8 image: simple-webapp-color
 9 envFrom:
10 - configMapRef:
11 name: app-secret

  • env

    1envFrom:
2 - secretRef:
3 name: app-config

  • single env

    1env:
2 - name: DB_Password
3 valueFrom:
4 secretKeyRef:
5 name: app-secret
6 key: DB_Password

  • volume

    1volumes:
2- name: app-secret-volumne
3 secret:
4 secretName: app-secret
    • inside the container
      • list 
        1ls /opt/app-secret-volumes
      • content 
        1cat /opt/app-secret-volumes/DB_Password

InitContainers

In multi-container pod, want to run a process that runs to completion in a container

  • initContainer

     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: myapp-pod
 5 labels:
 6 app: myapp
 7spec:
 8 containers:
 9 - name: myapp-container
10 image: busybox:1.28
11 command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo The app is running! && sleep 3600']
12 initContainers:
13 - name: init-myservice
14 image: busybox
15 command: ['sh', '-c', 'git clone <some-repository-that-will-be-used-by-application> ; done;']

  • multiple initContainers

     1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: myapp-pod
 5 labels:
 6 app: myapp
 7spec:
 8 containers:
 9 - name: myapp-container
10 image: busybox:1.28
11 command: ['sh', '-c', 'echo The app is running! && sleep 3600']
12 initContainers:
13 - name: init-myservice
14 image: busybox:1.28
15 command: ['sh', '-c', 'until nslookup myservice; do echo waiting for myservice; sleep 2; done;']
16 - name: init-mydb
17 image: busybox:1.28
18 command: ['sh', '-c', 'until nslookup mydb; do echo waiting for mydb; sleep 2; done;']

item.title: \[CKA\] 03. Logging & Monitoring
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

Metrics Server

  • In-memory
  • does not store

Install

  • minikube
    • minikube addons enable metrics-server
  • others
    • git clone <https://github.con/kubernetes-incubator/metrics-server>
    • kubectl create -f deploy/1.8+

View

  • kubeclt top node
  • kubeclt top pod

Managing Application Logs

Logs - k8s

로그 보기 w\ defintion

  • kubectl create -f sample.yaml
  • kubectl logs -f sample.yaml

여러 container

  • kubectl logs -f sample.yaml specific-container

item.title: \[CKA\] 02. Scheduler
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

Node name

  • default: kubectl-scheduler
  • 위 작업이 기본적으로 pod을 node에 할당해 줌

상태 확인

  • kubectl get node

  • node = no

    1> k get no
2NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION
3controlplane Ready control-plane,master 11m v1.20.0
4node01 Ready <none> 9m48s v1.20.0

수동 배정

  • To schedule node manually
  • NodeName 
     1---
 2apiVersion: v1
 3kind: Pod
 4metadata:
 5 name: nginx
 6spec:
 7 containers:
 8 -  image: nginx
 9 name: nginx
10 nodeName: node01

Label & Selectors

Label

  • definition

    1apiVersion: ~~
2kind: ~~
3metadata:
4 name: ~~
5 labels:
6 app: App1
7 function: Front-end
8sepc: ~~

    → on metadata: labels

  • imperative

    1kubectl label node node01 key=value

Select

  • kubectl get pods --selector app=App1
  • several selector field
    • kubectl get pods --selector app=App1,env=dev

Replicaset

  • replica-definition.yaml 
     1apiVersion: apps/v1
 2kind: Replcaset
 3metadata:
 4 name: simple-webapp
 5 labels:
 6 app: App1
 7 function: Front-end
 8spec:
 9 replicas: 3
10 selector:
11 matchLabels:
12 app: App1
13 template:
14 metadata:
15 labels:
16 app: App1
17 function: Front-end
18 spec:
19 containers:
20 - name: simple-webapp
21 image: simple-webapp
  • labels at the top are the labels of the replicaset itself. 
    1apiVersion: apps/v1
2kind: Replcaset
3metadata:
4 name: simple-webapp
5 labels:
6 app: App1
7 function: Front-end
  • in order to connect the replicaset to the pod, configure the selector field 
    1 selector:
2 matchLabels:
3 app: App1

Annotations

  • used to record other detail for informatively purpose
  • eg) tool details

Taints, Tolerations

  • taints
    • set on the nodes
  • toleartions
    • set on the pods
  • taints and toleration does not tell the pod to go to a particular node
  • node to only accept pod with certain tolerations

Taints

설정

  • kubectl taint nodes node-name key=value:taint-effect
  • taint-effect :
    • NoSchedule
      • not to be scheduled, if they do not tolerate the taint
    • PreferNoSchedule
      • system will be try to avoid placing a pod on the node, not guaranteed
    • NoExecute
      • new pods will not be scheduled on the node
      • existing pods on the node will be evicted if the do not tolertae the taint

확인

  • describe & grep 
    1k describe node node01 |grep -i taints
2Taints: <none>

제거

  • kubectl taint nodes node-name key=value:taint-effect- 
    1kubectl taint nodes master/controlplane node-role.kubernetes.io/master:NoSchedule-

Tolerations

설정

 1apiVersion:
 2kind: Pod
 3metadata:
 4 name: myapp-pod
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name: nginx-container
 8 - image: nginx
 9 tolerations:
10 - key: "app"
11 operator: "Equal"
12 value: "blue"
13 effect: "NoSchedule"

PODs to Node

  1. Node Selector
  2. Node Affinity

Node Selector

  • Label nodes

  • use nodeSelector with label

     1apiVersion:
 2kind:
 3metadata:
 4 name:
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name:
 8 image:
 9 nodeSelector:
10 size: Large

  • limitations

    • only use single label
    • there should be complex constraints

Node Affinity

  • nodeSelector vs affinity
    • two are working equally, schedule pods to Large node.

설정

 1apiVersion:
 2kind:
 3metadata:
 4 name:
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name:
 8 image:
 9 affinity:
10 nodeAffinity:
11 requireDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
12 nodeSelectorTerms:
13 - matchExpressions:
14 - key:
15 operator:
16 values:
  • operator
    • In
      • guarantee to schedule in values
    • NotIn
      • guarantee not to schedule in values
    • Exists
      • given key exists or not

Node Affinity Types

  • available
    • requireDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
    • preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution
  • planned
    • requireDuringSchedulingRequiredDuringExecution
  • two states in the life cycle of pod
    • DuringScheduling
      • Required
        • must be
      • Preferred
        • best to
    • DuringExecution
      • Ignored
        • if they are scheduled, will not impact them.
      • Required
        • pod will be evicted

Resource Limits

Resource Requests

설정

 1apiVersion:
 2kind:
 3metadata:
 4 name:
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name:
 8 image:
 9 resources:
10 requests:
11 memory: "1Gi"
12 cpu: 1
  • cpu:
    • 0.1 == 100mi
    • lower: 1mi
  • memory

Resources Limits

설정

 1apiVersion:
 2kind:
 3metadata:
 4 name:
 5spec:
 6 containers:
 7 - name:
 8 image:
 9 resources:
10 requests:
11 memory: "1Gi"
12 cpu: 1
13 limits:
14 memory: "2Gi"
15 cpu: 2
  • default
    • 1v CPU
    • 512Mi

Daemon Sets

  • one copy of the pod is always present in all nodes in the cluster

use case

  • monitoring
  • kube-proxy
  • networking

생성

  • DaemonSet vs ReplicaSet

  • definition

     1apiVersion: apps/v1
 2kind: DaemonSet
 3metadata:
 4 name: monitoring-daemon
 5spec:
 6 selector:
 7 matchLabels:
 8 app: monitoring-daemon
 9 template:
10 metadata:
11 labels:
12 app: monitoring-daemon
13 spec:
14 contaiers:
15 - name: monitoring-agent
16 image: monitoring-agent

상태

  • kubectl get daemonsets

Static PODs

Static PODs vs DaemonSets

  • static pod
    • created directly by kubelet

Config

  1. --pod-manifeset-path=/etc/Kubernetes/manifest
  2. --config=kubeconfig.yaml 
    1# kubeconfig.yaml
2staticPodPath: /etc/Kubernetes/manifest

파일 위치

First idenity the kubelet config file:

1root@controlplane:~# ps -aux | grep /usr/bin/kubelet
2root 3668 0.0 1.5 1933476 63076 ? Ssl Mar13 16:18 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2
3root 4879 0.0 0.0 11468 1040 pts/0 S+ 00:06 0:00 grep --color=auto /usr/bin/kubelet
4root@controlplane:~#

From the output we can see that the kubelet config file used is /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml

Next, lookup the value assigned for staticPodPath:

1root@controlplane:~# grep -i staticpod /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml
2staticPodPath: /etc/kubernetes/manifests
3root@controlplane:~#

As you can see, the path configured is the /etc/kubernetes/manifests directory.

상태

  • kubectl get po -A
    • -controlplane appended pods
     1> k get po -A
 2NAMESPACE NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE
 3kube-system coredns-74ff55c5b-578df 1/1 Running 0 25m
 4kube-system coredns-74ff55c5b-nnjdp 1/1 Running 0 25m
 5kube-system etcd-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 25m
 6kube-system kube-apiserver-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 25m
 7kube-system kube-controller-manager-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 25m
 8kube-system kube-flannel-ds-q7plw 1/1 Running 0 24m
 9kube-system kube-flannel-ds-w5rnm 1/1 Running 0 25m
10kube-system kube-proxy-5dg8f 1/1 Running 0 24m
11kube-system kube-proxy-ld2qq 1/1 Running 0 25m
12kube-system kube-scheduler-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 25m
    • grep -i controlplane
    1> k get po -A |grep -i controlplane
2kube-system etcd-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 27m
3kube-system kube-apiserver-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 27m
4kube-system kube-controller-manager-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 27m
5kube-system kube-scheduler-controlplane 1/1 Running 0 27m

삭제

First, let's identify the node in which the pod called static-greenbox is created. To do this, run:

1root@controlplane:~# kubectlget pods --all-namespaces -o wide | grep static-greenbox
2defaultstatic-greenbox-node01 1/1Running 0 19s 10.244.1.2 node01 <none> <none>
3root@controlplane:~#

From the result of this command, we can see that the pod is running on node01.

Next, SSH to node01 and identify the path configured for static pods in this node.

Important: The path need not be /etc/kubernetes/manifests.

Make sure to check the path configured in the kubelet configuration file.

1root@controlplane:~# ssh node01
2root@node01:~# ps -ef | grep /usr/bin/kubelet
3root 752 654 0 00:30 pts/0 00:00:00 grep --color=auto /usr/bin/kubelet
4root 28567 1 0 00:22 ? 00:00:11 /usr/bin/kubelet --bootstrap-kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/bootstrap-kubelet.conf --kubeconfig=/etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf --config=/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml --network-plugin=cni --pod-infra-container-image=k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2
5root@node01:~# grep -i staticpod /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml
6staticPodPath: /etc/just-to-mess-with-you
7root@node01:~#

Here the staticPodPath is /etc/just-to-mess-with-you

Navigate to this directory and delete the YAML file:

1root@node01:/etc/just-to-mess-with-you# ls
2greenbox.yaml
3root@node01:/etc/just-to-mess-with-you# rm -rf greenbox.yaml
4root@node01:/etc/just-to-mess-with-you#

Exit out of node01 using CTRL + D or type exit. You should return to the controlplane node. Check if the static-greenbox pod has been deleted:

1root@controlplane:~# kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o wide | grep static-greenbox
2root@controlplane:~#

Multiple Schedulers

생성

  • cli
  • kubeadm
    • --leader-elect
      • used when multiple copies of the scheduler running on different master nodes
      • who will lead activity
    • --scheduler-name
      • name of scheduler
    • --lock-object-name

사용

  • definition 
    1apiVersion: v1
2kind: Pod
3metadata:
4 name: nginx
5spec:
6 containers:
7 - image: nginx
8 name: nginx
9 schedulerName: my-custom-scheduler

상태

  • kubectl get events
  • kubectl logs my-custom-scheduler --n kube-system

item.title: \[CKA\] 02. Core Concepts
item.description:
CKA를 준비하면서 공부한 요약 내용입니다.

  • 강의
  • What is CKA?

0. Tips

  1. ailias kubectl

    1> alias k="kubectl"
2> k get po
3No resources found in default namespace.

  2. shortcuts

    • pod = po
    • service = svc
    • namespace = ns
    • replicasets = rs

  3. do not write all config file

    • use dry run kubectl run nginx --image=nginx --dry-run=client -o yaml kubectl create deployment --image=nginx nginx --dry-run=client -o yaml
    • export it to yaml file kubectl create deployment --image=nginx nginx --dry-run=client -o yaml > nginx-deployment.yaml

1. Pods

생성

  1. yaml
 1# pod.yaml
 2apiVersion: v1
 3kind: pod
 4metadata:
 5 name: nginx
 6spec:
 7 containers:
 8 - name: nginx-container
 9 image: nginx
10kubectl apply -f pod.yaml
  1. run
1kubectl run nginx --image=nginx

상태

  • 기본

    1> k get po
2NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE
3newpods-6nl8r 1/1 Running 0 3m
4newpods-9sp8p 1/1 Running 0 3m
5newpods-tplx9 1/1 Running 0 3m
6nginx 1/1 Running 0 3m11s
7webapp 1/2 ImagePullBackOff 0 54s
    • READY means
      • running_containers in pod / total_containers in pod

  • wide

    1> k get po -o wide
2NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE IP NODE NOMINATED NODE READINESS GATES
3newpods-6nl8r 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 14s <none> controlplane <none> <none>
4newpods-9sp8p 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 14s <none> controlplane <none> <none>
5newpods-tplx9 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 14s <none> controlplane <none> <none>
6nginx 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 25s <none> controlplane <none> <none>
    • NODE

  • describe

     1> k describe po webapp
 2Name: webapp
 3Namespace: default
 4Priority: 0
 5Node: controlplane/10.77.67.9
 6Start Time: Mon, 10 May 2021 03:54:38 +0000
 7Labels: <none>
 8Annotations: <none>
 9Status: Pending
10IP: 10.244.0.8
11IPs:
12 IP: 10.244.0.8
13Containers:
14 nginx:
15 Container ID: docker://f9580f7e4b7c51b53f0cb0d94ff913b643706a65a103a58614c3c254cf26043f
16 Image: nginx
17 Image ID: docker-pullable://nginx@sha256:75a55d33ecc73c2a242450a9f1cc858499d468f077ea942867e662c247b5e412
18 Port: <none>
19 Host Port: <none>
20 State: Running
21 Started: Mon, 10 May 2021 03:54:41 +0000
22 Ready: True
23 Restart Count: 0
24 Environment: <none>
25 Mounts:
26 /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from default-token-pz92t (ro)
27 agentx:
28 Container ID:
29 Image: agentx
30 Image ID:
31 Port: <none>
32 Host Port: <none>
33 State: Waiting
34 Reason: ImagePullBackOff
35 Ready: False
36 Restart Count: 0
37 Environment: <none>
38 Mounts:
39 /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount from default-token-pz92t (ro)
40Conditions:
41 Type Status
42 Initialized True
43 Ready False
44 ContainersReady False
45 PodScheduled True
46Volumes:
47 default-token-pz92t:
48 Type: Secret (a volume populated by a Secret)
49 SecretName: default-token-pz92t
50 Optional: false
51QoS Class: BestEffort
52Node-Selectors: <none>
53Tolerations: node.kubernetes.io/not-ready:NoExecute op=Exists for 300s
54 node.kubernetes.io/unreachable:NoExecute op=Exists for 300s
55Events:
56 Type Reason Age From Message
57 ---- ------ ---- ---- -------
58 Normal Scheduled 17s default-scheduler Successfully assigned default/webapp to controlplane
59 Normal Pulling 15s kubelet Pulling image "nginx"
60 Normal Pulled 15s kubelet Successfully pulled image "nginx" in 170.242045ms
61 Normal Created 15s kubelet Created container nginx
62 Normal Started 14s kubelet Started container nginx
63 Normal Pulling 14s kubelet Pulling image "agentx"
64 Warning Failed 13s kubelet Failed to pull image "agentx": rpc error: code = Unknown desc = Error response from daemon: pull access denied for agentx, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login': denied: requested access to the resource is denied
65 Warning Failed 13s kubelet Error: ErrImagePull
66 Normal BackOff 12s (x2 over 13s) kubelet Back-off pulling image "agentx"
67 Warning Failed 12s (x2 over 13s) kubelet Error: ImagePullBackOff

삭제

1root@controlplane:~# k delete po webapp
2pod "webapp" deleted

수정

1> k edit po redis

2. Replication Controller

What is replica and why need controller?

  • replication controller
    • run multiple instances of a single pod in the k8s cluster

특징

  1. high availability
    • multiplie pod or single pod
    • replication controller ensures that the specified number of pods are running at all times.
  2. Load Balancing & Scaling
    • balance the load
    • spans across multiple nodes
  • replication controller
    • old technology
  • replica set
    • recommended

생성

  • defintion 
     1# ReplicaSet
 2apiVersion: apps/v1
 3kind: ReplicaSet
 4metadata:
 5 name: myapp-replicaset
 6 labels:
 7 app: myapp
 8 type: front-end
 9spec:
10 template:
11 metadata: myapp-pod
12 name: myapp-pod
13 labels:
14 app: myapp
15 type: front-end
16 spec:
17 containers:
18 - name: nginx-container
19 image: nginx
20 replicas: 3
21 selector:
22 matchLabels:
23 type: front-end

상태

1> k get replicasets
2NAME DESIRED CURRENT READY AGE
3new-replica-set 4 4 0 11s

수정

1> k edit replicaset
2replicaset.apps/new-replica-set edited

삭제

1> k delete po --all

-> 자동으로 다시 pod 생성 됨

 1ot@controlplane:~# k get po
 2NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE
 3new-replica-set-7mx8k 0/1 Terminating 0 4m22s
 4new-replica-set-7p95n 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 17s
 5new-replica-set-dcl5m 0/1 Terminating 0 4m22s
 6new-replica-set-hhpm9 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 17s
 7new-replica-set-hr54x 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 17s
 8new-replica-set-m6dmb 0/1 Terminating 0 4m22s
 9new-replica-set-v2vr5 0/1 ContainerCreating 0 17s
10new-replica-set-vmts5 0/1 Terminating 0 4m22s

scale

  1. replace w\ file 
    1kubectl replace -f ~~.yaml
  2. scale w\ file 
    1kubectl scale --replicas=6 -f ~~.yaml
  3. scale w\ resource 
    1kubectl scale --replicas=6 replicaset myapp-replicaset
  4. edit 
    1kubectl edit replicaset
  • 실습 
    1> k scale --replicas=5 rs new-replica-set
2replicaset.apps/new-replica-set scaled

3. Deployments

  • rolling updates
  • rolling back

4. Namespaces

  • user의 실수부터 보호하기 위해서 처음 3개의 namespace가 생성됨
    • Default
    • kube-system
    • kube-public
  • isolate resources between namespace
  • own policies
    • each namespace is guaranteed certain amount and does not to use more.
  • to reach in same namespace
    • mysql.connect("db-service")
  • to reach in other namespace
    • mysql.connect(db-service.dev.svc.cluster.local)
    • {service-name}.{namespace}.{service}-{domain-name}

생성

1k create ns dev-ns
2namespace/dev-ns created

목록

 1> k get ns
 2
 3NAME STATUS AGE
 4default Active 88s
 5dev Active 34s
 6finance Active 34s
 7kube-node-lease Active 93s
 8kube-public Active 94s
 9kube-system Active 95s
10manufacturing Active 33s
11marketing Active 34s
12prod Active 33s
13research Active 33s

상태

  1. --namespace 
    1kubectl get pods --namespace=kube-system
  2. -n 
    1kubectl get pods -n kube-system

change default

1kubectl config set-context $(kubectl config current-context) --namespace=dev

pod 생성

1k run redis --image=redis --namespace=finance
2pod/redis created

전체 확인

1k get po --all-namespaces

  • 하나의 값만 찾고 싶을 때

    1k get po --all-namespaces | grep -i blue

5. Services

allow to communicate w\ people, backend, frontend, extra data source

types

  • node port
    • make an internal pod accessible on a port on node
  • cluster ip
    • creates a virtual IP inside the cluster to enable communication b2n different services
  • load balancer
    • provisions a load balancer for service for cloud provider

NodePort

  • target port
  • port
    • port of service itself
  • node port
    • valid range: 30000 ~ 32767
 1apiVersion: v1
 2kind: SErvice
 3metadata:
 4 name: myapp-service
 5spec:
 6 type: NodePort
 7 ports:
 8 - targetPort: 80
 9 port: 80
10 nodePort: 30008
  • if targetPort is not given
    • same as port
  • if nodePort is not given
    • automatically allocated
  • randomly distributed if same port is exists

ClusterIP

LoadBalancer

상태

1> k get svc
2
3NAME TYPE CLUSTER-IP EXTERNAL-IP PORT(S) AGE
4kubernetes ClusterIP 10.96.0.1 <none> 443/TCP 3m46s

명령어

1> kubectl expose pod redis --port=6379 --name redis-service

expose

1> kubectl run custom-nginx --image=nginx --port=8080
2kubectl run httpd --image=httpd:alpine --port=80 --expose

6. Imperative vs Declaritive

Infrastructure as Code

Kubernetes

Imperative Commands

  • create objects
  • update objects → hard to keep track

Imperative Configuration Files

  • create objects
    • kubectl create -f ~.yaml
  • update obejcts
    • kubectl edit ~~ ~~
    • kubectl replace -f ~.yaml

Declaritve

  • create objects
    • kubectl apply -f ~.yaml
    • kubectl apply -f /path/to/config-files
  • update objects
    • kubectl apply -f ~.yaml

item.title: kubernetes에서 ghcr package pull 하기
item.description:
kubernetes 에서 사용하는 image는 기본적으로 dockerhub에서 pull 된다. 기본적으로 Local에 있는 패키지는 사용하지 않는다. 회사에서는 도커 이미지를 dockerhub와 같은 곳에 public 하게 공유할 수가 없다. 그래서 github 에서 제공하는 (GHCR) GitHub Container Registry for Docker images 을 이용해서 도커 이미지를 관리하고 있다.

GHCR 을 이용하기 위해서는 github access key 가 필요하다. 우선 github access token을 발급 받아 보자.

1. ghcr 을 위한 github access token 발급 받기

우선 github setting에서 deplotver settings / personal access tokens 로 들어간다.

여기서 generate new token을 누른다.

토큰 이름으로 ghcr-token을 입력하고 packages와 관련된 권한을 주고 token을 생성한다.

다음과 같이 토큰이 생성된다. 이제 이 토큰을 잘 저장해둔다.

2. kubernetes secret

다음으로 kubernetes 에 secret 을 생성해준다.

1kubectl -n <k8s-namespace> create secret docker-registry <k8s-docker-registry-secret-name> \
2 --docker-server=ghcr.io \
3 --docker-username=<github-username> \
4 --docker-password=<github-personal-access-token> \
5 --docker-email=<email-address>
  • k8s-docker-registry-secret-name: secret 이름
  • github-username: github 유저 네임
  • github-personal-access-token: 발급받은 토큰
  • email-address: github 이메일

예를 들면 아래와 같이 사용할 수 있다.

1> kubectl -n default create secret docker-registry test-ghcr \
2 --docker-server=ghcr.io \
3 --docker-username=aiden-jeon \
4 --docker-password=<github-personal-access-token> \
5 --docker-email=ells2124@gmail.com

정상적으로 실행될 경우 아래와 같이 출력된다.

1secret/test-ghcr created

3. yaml 파일 작성

이제 deployment를 위한 yaml 파일을 작성한다.

 1apiVersion: apps/v1
 2kind: Deployment
 3metadata:
 4 name: test-deploy
 5spec:
 6 selector:
 7 matchLabels:
 8 type: app
 9 service: test-deploy
10 template:
11 metadata:
12 labels:
13 type: app
14 service: test-deploy
15 spec:
16 containers:
17 - name: test-deploy
18 image: ghcr.io/{user-name}/{package-name}:{package-tag}
19 imagePullPolicy: Always
20 ports:
21 - containerPort: 80
22 imagePullSecrets:
23 - name: test-ghcr

image에 ghcr 의 도커 이미지를 입력해주면 된다. 그리고 imagePullSecrets에 좀 전에 생성한 secret 의 이름을 입력하면 된다.
